skill.name: draw
skill.description: Vector graphics and diagram creation, format conversion (ODG/SVG/PDF) with LibreOffice Draw.
skill.summary: Create vector graphics and convert diagrams between formats using LibreOffice Draw command-line tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation skill for LibreOffice Draw. Static findings flagged external commands, network URLs, and weak crypto patterns, but evaluation confirms these are all false positives. The skill contains only documentation and example commands for using LibreOffice's legitimate graphics tools - no actual code execution, no malicious behavior, and no security risks.

content.user_title: Create Vector Graphics with LibreOffice Draw
content.value_statement: Convert diagrams between ODG, SVG, PDF, and PNG formats using LibreOffice Draw command-line tools for automated graphics workflows.
actual_capabilities: Create new ODG drawings from templates
Convert ODG files to SVG, PDF, PNG, and JPG formats
Convert SVG and PDF files to ODG format
Automate diagram creation with command-line tools
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installed on the system
Does not support real-time interactive editing via skill
Limited to formats supported by LibreOffice Draw
Some complex vector effects may not convert perfectly

use_cases.title: Automate Diagram Format Conversion
use_cases.description: Convert exported diagrams from ODG to web-friendly SVG or PDF for documentation

use_cases.title: Batch Process Graphics Files
use_cases.description: Convert multiple ODG files to PNG or PDF in bulk for distribution

use_cases.title: Generate Technical Drawings
use_cases.description: Create flowcharts and technical diagrams from templates using CLI automation

prompt_templates.title: Convert Diagram to PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert my diagram file diagram.odg to PDF format using LibreOffice Draw
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting a single ODG file to PDF

prompt_templates.title: Batch Convert to SVG
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert all ODG files in the current folder to SVG format
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch converting multiple files to SVG

prompt_templates.title: Create Flowchart from Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a flowchart using the template at template.odg and export to PNG

prompt_templates.title: Export High-Resolution PNG
prompt_templates.prompt: Export my drawing.odg to PNG at 2048x2048 resolution

output_examples.output: The file has been converted to diagram.svg. You can now use the SVG in web projects or further edit in vector graphics software.

output_examples.input: Batch convert all *.odg files to PDF
output_examples.output: Successfully converted 5 files: diagram1.odg → diagram1.pdf, diagram2.odg → diagram2.pdf, and 3 more files.

best_practices: Keep ODG source files for editing and export to other formats as needed
Use templates for recurring diagram types to maintain consistency
Test conversions with complex vector graphics before batch processing
Export to SVG for web use and PDF for print-ready documents
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to edit complex drawings interactively through command line
Avoid converting raster images to vector expecting perfect results
Do not skip testing conversions before processing large batches

faq.question: What is ODG format?
faq.answer: ODG is the OpenDocument Graphics format used by LibreOffice Draw. It is an open standard for vector graphics.

faq.question: Does this skill require LibreOffice installed?
faq.answer: Yes, LibreOffice must be installed on the system for the command-line tools to work.

faq.question: Can I convert SVG to ODG?
faq.answer: Yes, you can convert SVG files to ODG format using the --convert-to odg command.

faq.question: How do I batch convert multiple files?
faq.answer: Use a shell loop or script to iterate through files and apply the soffice --convert-to command to each.

faq.question: What is the best format for web graphics?
faq.answer: SVG is best for web graphics as it scales without quality loss and has small file sizes.

faq.question: Can I control output resolution for PNG export?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --filterData parameter with Width and Height values to specify resolution.
